mainEntity.name: How do I create a CSS gradient?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use our CSS Gradient Generator above: pick your colors, choose gradient type (linear, radial, or conic), set the direction/angle, and copy the generated CSS code. Example output: background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea, #764ba2);

mainEntity.name: What is the CSS syntax for a gradient background?

acceptedAnswer.text: The basic syntax is: background: linear-gradient(direction, color1, color2); For example: background: linear-gradient(to right, #ff6b6b, #feca57); You can add multiple color stops and use percentages to control transitions.

mainEntity.name: Can I use gradients with transparency in CSS?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, use rgba() or hsla() color values for transparent gradients. Example: background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(0,0,0,0), rgba(0,0,0,0.8)); This creates a fade-to-black overlay commonly used on hero images.
